questions.question: State the Pumping Lemma for Regular Languages and explain each component clearly.
keyPoints: If L is regular, ∃p > 0 such that ∀w ∈ L with |w| ≥ p, w can be written as xyz
|xy| ≤ p (pumping window within first p symbols)
|y| > 0 (pumped part is non-empty)
xyⁱz ∈ L for all i ≥ 0 (any number of pumps keeps string in language)

questions.question: Using Pumping Lemma, prove that L = {aⁿbⁿ | n ≥ 0} is not regular.
keyPoints: Assume L is regular with pumping length p
Choose w = aᵖbᵖ (w ∈ L, |w| ≥ p)
By Pumping Lemma, w = xyz with |xy| ≤ p and |y| > 0
Since |xy| ≤ p, y must be aᵏ for some k ≥ 1
Pumping DOWN (i=0): xz = aᵖ⁻ᵏbᵖ ∉ L because p-k ≠ p
This contradicts Pumping Lemma, hence L is not regular

questions.question: State and explain Myhill-Nerode Theorem for proving regularity.
keyPoints: Language L is regular iff the equivalence relation R_L has finite index
x R_L y iff ∀z ∈ Σ*, (xz ∈ L ⇔ yz ∈ L)
Index = number of equivalence classes
Finite index means finite distinguishable sets → can be recognized by DFA with finite states

questions.question: Prove that L = {ww | w ∈ {a,b}*} is not regular using Myhill-Nerode Theorem.
keyPoints: Find infinite set of pairwise distinguishable strings
Consider strings: a, aa, aaa, aaaa, ...
For any two different strings aⁱ and aʲ (i ≠ j)
Choose suffix z = aⁱ: aⁱaⁱ ∈ L but aʲaⁱ ∉ L (or vice versa)
Thus each aⁿ is distinguishable from all others
Infinite distinguishable strings → infinite index → not regular

questions.question: What is meant by 'distinguishable' strings in Myhill-Nerode context? Give an example.
keyPoints: Two strings x and y are distinguishable wrt L if ∃z such that exactly one of xz and yz ∈ L
Example: In L = {aⁿbⁿ}, strings a and bb are distinguishable
Choose z = b: ab ∈ L but bbb ∉ L (or aab vs b: aab ∈ L, b ∉ L)
Distinguishable strings cannot be merged in DFA states

questions.question: Explain pumping UP and pumping DOWN techniques in Pumping Lemma proofs.
keyPoints: Pumping UP: Show xy²z (or xyⁱz for i>1) ∉ L
Pumping DOWN: Show xz (i=0) ∉ L by removing y
Both approaches valid - aim to show contradiction
Example for aⁿbⁿ: pumping down removes some a's making count mismatch

questions.question: How can closure properties be used to prove a language is not regular? Explain with an example.
keyPoints: If L is non-regular and R is regular, L ∩ R or L ∪ R may help
Or if operation on regular languages yields non-regular result
Example: L₁ = {aⁿbⁿ} is non-regular
L₂ = a*b* is regular
But L₁ ∩ a*b* = {aⁿbⁿ} still non-regular, doesn't help
Better: If regular ∩ L gives known non-regular, then L non-regular
